meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome the no portfolio trap. An intermediate guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first app.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' trap, bypass boilerplate fatigue by using a high-fidelity SaaS starter kit. By leveraging AI-driven tools like Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can ship a production-ready, feature-complete SaaS application in days, transforming your GitHub profile from empty to elite.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a new repository is the graveyard of ambition. You have the skills, you have the vision, but the lack of a tangible, shipping product keeps you stuck in the 'tutorial hell' loop, invisible to recruiters and potential collaborators who only care about what you’ve actually built.

It is time to stop building todo apps and start building businesses. By treating a SaaS starter kit as your foundation, you aren't just writing code—you are architecting a professional-grade ecosystem. This is your bridge from student to engineer, turning your technical potential into a portfolio that commands respect.

steps.title: Architecting the Foundation
steps.description: Define the core SaaS architecture including authentication, database schema, and payment integration. A strong foundation prevents future refactoring.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable SaaS architecture for a subscription-based task manager using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Include a folder structure that separates UI, API routes, and service logic.

steps.title: Implementing Robust Authentication
steps.description: Secure your app immediately. Using a starter kit handles the complex edge cases of JWTs and session persistence so you don't have to.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure middleware implementation for Supabase Auth in Next.js that protects dashboard routes and handles redirect logic for unauthenticated users.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement protected routes in middleware.ts and auth-helpers'

steps.title: Integrating Payment Gateways
steps.description: The hallmark of a real SaaS is the ability to accept money. We configure the webhook handlers to ensure subscription status stays in sync.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler that listens for checkout.session.completed and updates the user's subscription status in the Supabase profiles table.

steps.title: Building the UI/UX Layer
steps.description: Use component libraries to ensure your app looks like it was built by a senior team. Focus on consistency and accessibility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive dashboard layout using Tailwind CSS and Radix UI primitives. Include a sidebar navigation, top bar with user profile, and a main content area.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'build responsive dashboard shell using shadcn/ui components'

steps.title: Deploying to Production
steps.description: Move from local development to a live URL. This is the moment your project becomes a real-world asset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file that includes environment variable validation and build-time secrets management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure vercel.json and verify production build constraints'

pro_tips: Always implement feature flags early; it allows you to test new logic in production without breaking the main user experience.
Treat your README.md as a product pitch, not just a technical doc—document the 'why' behind your design choices for potential employers.
Use database migrations instead of manual schema changes to ensure your production data remains consistent as your app evolves.
Prioritize error monitoring (like Sentry) over adding new features; a stable app is worth more than ten buggy ones.
Automate your testing pipeline with simple integration tests; recruiters value testing habits over raw lines of code.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, add a 'Case Study' section to your portfolio site that details the specific technical challenges you solved during this build, such as handling Stripe webhooks or managing complex database relations.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student following tutorials; you are a builder shipping products. This starter kit is your unfair advantage in a crowded market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
